_note: c.4000-3900 BC slab: the named twin-sisters of Cain and Abel from extra-biblical tradition, appended as further daughters of the existing ADAM ● EVE union. Genesis names no sisters; every person here is flagged as tradition with its source. Deliberately NOT added: a second Cain marriage (the spine already weds CAIN to AWAN per Jubilees 4:9, so Aclima is noted as the same wife-role under other traditions rather than given a contradictory union), a marriage for Abel (tradition has him slain young and childless, the quarrel being OVER a sister), and any unnamed extra daughters (07j already carries the Gen 5:4 'other sons & daughters' catch-all; named+sourced people are preferred). Sisters kept distinct from the existing AZURA (Seth's wife in Jubilees) to avoid conflating identities.

children.label:
Aclima
c.3990 BC · Cain's twin sister & wife in Syriac & Islamic tradition
· 'Qelima' in the Cave of Treasures; 'Luluwa' in the Conflict of Adam & Eve with Satan
· cf. Awan in Jubilees 4:9 · tradition, not named in Genesis

Balbira
c.3980 BC · Abel's twin sister · named in the Jewish Seder Hadorot
· Genesis Rabbah 22 reads Abel as born with two twin sisters, the quarrel over a sister leading to his death
· tradition, not named in Genesis
